Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specifications: Failed to advise radio dispatcher (Manual of Rules 4.10) – Failed to assist an
outside police agency (General Police Order 4.03.01) – Failed to act (Manual of Rules 4.11) –
Diminish the esteem (Manual of Rules 5.01) – Wearable Camera System (WCS) (failed to place
into event mode) (General Police Order 4.06.04) – Emergency Response Driving violation
(General Police Order 4.01.01).
Disposition: The emergency response driving charge was dismissed. The charges of failing to
assist an outside agency and failing to act were merged into one charge. The charges of failing to
advise radio, failing to assist an outside agency/failing to act and diminishing the esteem more
closely comport with Group I Violations (conduct that has a negative impact on the operations or
professional image of the Division or that negatively impacts relationships with other officers,
agencies or the public); the charges were amended from Group II violations to Group I
violations. Found “Guilty” of four Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none), the member received a three-day suspension. This corrective action is
within the First Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors
(multiple violations) outweigh mitigating factors (no active discipline).

Rationale: The member was en route to a Priority-2 assignment when he came upon a multi-
vehicle accident that blocked the lanes of West Saint Clair Avenue between West 3rd and West

6
th Streets. Two Strongsville Police vehicles were on scene with their emergency overhead lights
activated. Instead of stopping to see if assistance was needed and notifying radio of this police
incident of a serious nature, the member or his partner activated the patrol car’s overhead
emergency lights and proceeded to make a U-turn and leave the scene. These actions were
covered in a story by Fox 8 news and would reasonably tend to diminish the esteem of the Division of Police or its personnel in the eyes of the public. At no time during his response to the
Priority-2 assignment did the member activate his Wearable Camera System. Because the
member and his partner were en route to a Priority-2 assignment, these actions, as noted, more
closely comport with Group I violations. The charges of failing to assist an outside agency and
failing to act are closely related and were merged into a single charge. The use of lights and
sirens relating to emergency response driving is dictated by Ohio Revised Code §4511.45, and
there is insufficient evidence to support a finding of guilt regarding the member’s response to the
Priority-2 assignment, and the charge was dismissed. Multiple violations arising from a single
event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the maximum group range.
Charges: Failure to Report/Notify,Failed to Assist,Lack of Service,Conduct Unbecoming / Diminished Esteem,WCS Violation,Travel Violation

Charge & Discipline Decision:
Specification: Failed to disclose knowledge of available Wearable Camera System video
(General Police Order 1.07.05).
Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction.
Rationale: The member completed a BlueTeam “DBI” (District/Bureau Investigation) for
forwarding through the chain of command regarding Workplace Violence. The member failed to
follow the Blueteam investigation template per policy and failed to include in the investigation
that a Wearable Camera System video recording, which covered a portion of the incident, was
uploaded to Evidence.com. Because the member instructed a change to the category of this video
in Evidence.com to a longer retention period, the failure to note the existence of the video in the
BlueTeam entry appears to be an oversight. Reinstruction is appropriate in this instance.

Specifications: Wearable Camera System (WCS) violation (General Police Order 4.06.04) –
Operated a Cleveland Police vehicle that was involved in a motor vehicle crash that was
reviewed by the Traffic Safety Analysis Panel (TSAP) and determined to be preventable
(Manual of Rules 4.03, 6.03 and 6.05).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of two Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a Written Reprimand for her second preventable
crash within two years. In addition, the member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of
Reinstruction for the remaining charge. This corrective action is within the Group I violation
range of the discipline matrix when mitigating factors (no active discipline) outweigh
aggravating factors (second preventable crash within two years).
Rationale: The member operated a patrol car while on-duty that was involved in a motor vehicle
crash in which the Traffic Safety Analysis Panel (TSAP) determined to be preventable. While
the lights and siren were activated, she failed to proceed through an intersection with due regard
for the safety of all persons and property. The member failed to activate her Wearable Camera
System while responding to a call for service as required.

Specifications: Unsafe operation of a patrol car (Manual of Rules 6.03) – Wearable Camera
System (WCS) violation (General Police Order 4.06.04).
Disposition: The member received a Non-disciplinary Letter of Reinstruction. In addition, the
member shall attend retraining with the Training Section regarding felony traffic stops.
Rationale: The member failed to activate his Wearable Camera System when conducting a
felony traffic stop. Additionally, he placed his patrol car in front of the subject vehicle during a
felony traffic stop. This unsafe operation of the patrol car placed himself and a probationary
patrol officer in a dangerous position in relation to the suspects. Reinstruction and retraining is
appropriate in this instance.

Specifications: Wearable Camera System (WCS) violation (×3) (General Police Order 4.06.04
and Divisional Notice 25-158) – Failed to notify radio dispatch (×2) (Manual of Rules 7.04) –
Used an electronic wireless communications device while operating a motor vehicle (×2) (Ohio
Revised Code 4511.204, Cleveland Codified Ordinance 431.34 and Manual of Rules 2.01) –
Operated a Cleveland Police vehicle that was involved in a motor vehicle crash that was
reviewed by the Traffic Safety Analysis Panel (TSAP) and determined to be preventable
(Manual of Rules 4.03, 6.03 and 6.05) – Initiated a vehicle pursuit that did not meet the approved
criteria and failed to advise the Communications Control Section (CCS) or a supervisor (General
Police Order 3.2.02) – Failed to clear assignment (General Police Order 7.01.02) – Emergency
response driving violation (General Police Order 4.01.01) – Failed to complete an entry in the
log each time a patrol rifle was placed in or removed from storage (General Police Order
4.06.08) – Stored a City Patrol Rifle in a personal locker (General Police Order 4.06.08).
Disposition: Found “Guilty” of thirteen Group I violations. Considering the member’s active
discipline history (none) the member received a Letter of Reinstruction for the preventable motor
vehicle crash and a five-day suspension for the remaining charges. This corrective action is
within the Group I violation range of the discipline matrix when aggravating factors (multiple
violations arising from a single event or incident may escalate the disciplinary action to the
maximum group range, created safety risk to the public and Divisional members) outweigh
mitigating factors (no active discipline, accepted partial responsibility).
Rationale: The member operated a patrol car while on-duty that was involved in a motor vehicle
crash in which the Traffic Safety Analysis Panel (TSAP) determined to be preventable. The
member failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) and in-car camera prior to taking
investigative or enforcement actions and did fail to notify the Communications Control Section
(CCS) while investigating a stolen vehicle on July 28, 2025. The member used a wireless
communications device (cell phone) while operating a motor vehicle to contact other Divisional
members on July 28, 2025, and July 30, 2025, while the City radio system was functioning
appropriately. The member initiated a vehicle pursuit that did not meet the approved criteria and
failed to advise the Communications Control Section (CCS) or a supervisor. The member failed
to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS) prior to taking investigative or enforcement
action with the public and failed to advise a radio dispatcher of his activities on July 30, 2025.
The member failed to clear a parking violator assignment that he was currently assigned while
being involved in a vehicle pursuit and engaged in emergency response driving without
emergency lights and audible siren activated on July 30, 2025. His operation while engaged in
emergency response driving included driving left of center, driving on the wrong side of the
street, over-taking traffic, and high rates of speed, creating a risk to the safety of the public and
other Divisional members. The member failed to activate his Wearable Camera System (WCS)
prior to responding to a call for service on July 31, 2025. In addition, the member failed to
complete an entry in the log each time a City patrol rifle was placed in or removed from storage
and did store a City patrol rifle in his personal locker.
Charges: WCS Violation,Failure to Report/Notify,Telecommunications Violation,Preventable Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA),Vehicle Pursuit Violation,Travel Violation,Improper Handling of Firearm
